methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Multiply Labs is a Series A-stage biotech and health company founded by Fred Parietti that has raised $41M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 11 companies: 3 public-market peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms at the same or adjacent stage, including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $85M total-raised proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields $170M. Peer-median methodology across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces an $805M valuation, with the peer set ranging from $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied without disclosed revenue. The three public biotech tickers show a median P/S of 7.4x, but Multiply Labs' revenue remains proprietary.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $144M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $488M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in peer outcomes and uncertainty around Multiply Labs' revenue trajectory relative to the comp set. Stage-progression yields a conservative anchor; peer medians suggest substantially higher value, though the six-company sample includes Series B firms with additional funding milestones achieved. Key uncertainties include clinical-stage progress, intellectual property scope, and burn rate relative to capital runway.
